articles.headline: Mixing Up Your Workouts Could Add Years to Your Life
articles.body: Harvard researchers tracked 111,000 adults for 30 years and found something surprising: it's not just how much you exercise that matters. It's how many different kinds you do. People who mixed walking, lifting, cycling, and other activities had a 19% lower risk of premature death compared to those who stuck to one type. And that benefit held no matter how much total time they spent moving. The takeaway is simple. Your body adapts to what you repeat. Give it new demands and it pays you back with more years.
articles.takeaway: Add one new type of movement to your week. A swim, a hike, a yoga class. Variety is literally life-extending.
articles.source: Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health / BMJ Medicine

articles.headline: Being Strong Is Its Own Longevity Superpower
articles.body: A University at Buffalo study published in JAMA Network Open followed more than 5,000 women aged 63 to 99 and found that muscle strength cut death risk dramatically on its own. For every 7 kilograms of grip strength, mortality risk dropped 12%. The strongest women lived significantly longer, even after accounting for aerobic fitness, inflammation, and how much they moved. Even women who did not hit standard exercise guidelines benefited if they were stronger. Strength is not just about looking fit. It turns out it is one of the most powerful biological levers you have.
articles.takeaway: Test yourself: Can you do 5 sit-to-stand chair raises in under 11 seconds? That number is linked to your odds of living longer. Start training for it.
articles.source: University at Buffalo / JAMA Network Open

articles.headline: Scientists Just Found the Brain Switch That Builds Muscle While You Sleep
articles.body: UC Berkeley researchers published a landmark study in Cell that finally explains why deep sleep is so critical for your body. They mapped the exact brain circuits that trigger growth hormone release during non-REM sleep, the stage you hit in the first two to three hours of the night. That hormone drives muscle repair, fat metabolism, bone density, and cognitive function. Here is the kicker: alcohol before bed, an inconsistent bedtime, or a warm room can disrupt this circuit and tank your growth hormone output. Your sleep is not downtime. It is maintenance.
articles.takeaway: Protect your first two to three hours of sleep. Consistent bedtime, cool room, no nightcap. That window is your body's biggest hormone pulse of the day.

product.name: LectroFan White Noise Machine
product.blurb: Blocks ambient noise so your sleep is actually restorative.

articles.headline: The AHA Just Issued a Wake-Up Call About Your Brain
articles.body: A new American Heart Association scientific statement published April 28 in the journal Stroke says brain health is not mostly genetic and it is not mostly about what happens when you get old. A growing body of evidence shows that mental health, sleep quality, diet, social connections, and physical activity shape how your brain functions and ages across your entire lifetime. The habits you build in your 30s and 40s are writing the story your brain tells in your 70s. Getting regular exercise, sleeping well, and managing stress have all shown consistent protective benefits.
articles.takeaway: Your brain is a long game. The lifestyle choices you make this week are investments in your cognitive health decades from now.

product.name: Outlive - Peter Attia Audiobook
product.blurb: The definitive book on living longer and better. Required reading for anyone who trains.

quote.author: Ralph Waldo Emerson
quote.context: 19th-century American philosopher and essayist whose writing on self-reliance and human potential still shapes how we think about living well.

workout.title: Press, Dip, Dominate
equipment: optional dumbbells or backpack for resistance
workout.warmup: Spend 3 minutes getting the shoulders and chest ready. Do 20 arm circles forward and back, 10 shoulder rolls, and 10 slow controlled push-ups with a 3-second descent. Your elbows should feel warm and your chest slightly activated before you hit set one.

exercises.name: Push-Up
exercises.notes: Hands just outside shoulder width. Keep your core braced and lower chest to within an inch of the floor. If full push-ups are too easy, elevate your feet on a chair. If too hard, drop to your knees and still focus on a slow descent.

exercises.name: Pike Push-Up
exercises.notes: Start in a downward dog position with hips high. Bend your elbows and lower your head toward the floor between your hands. This hammers the shoulders hard. Keep your neck neutral and do not let your hips drop.

exercises.name: Tricep Dip
exercises.notes: Use a chair or low bench. Hands on the edge, fingers forward, feet extended in front of you. Lower until your elbows hit 90 degrees then press back up. Keep your back close to the chair throughout.

exercises.name: Wide Push-Up
exercises.notes: Place hands wider than normal, maybe 6 to 8 inches outside shoulders. This shifts more load to the chest. Lower slowly over 3 counts and explode up. Rest 60 seconds between sets.

exercises.name: Diamond Push-Up
exercises.notes: Form a diamond shape with your thumbs and index fingers directly under your chest. This is a tricep burner. If the wrist angle feels uncomfortable, shift your hands slightly apart until it feels clean.

workout.cooldown: Spend 2 minutes stretching. Hold a doorway chest stretch for 30 seconds on each side, then reach one arm across your chest and hold for 20 seconds per side to open the rear delt. Shake your hands out gently to release wrist tension.

meal.title: Greek Chicken Power Bowl
ingredients: 170g cooked chicken breast, sliced or shredded
1/2 cup cooked brown rice or quinoa
1/2 cup canned chickpeas, drained and rinsed
meal.instructions: 1. Cook or reheat your chicken breast and season with oregano, lemon juice, salt, and pepper. 2. Add the brown rice or quinoa to your bowl as the base, then layer on the chickpeas, tomatoes, cucumber, and spinach if using. 3. Place the sliced chicken on top, then add a scoop of hummus and a dollop of Greek yogurt on the side. 4. Squeeze extra lemon over the whole bowl and serve immediately.
meal.tip: Batch cook chicken and grains on Sunday and you can assemble this bowl in under 3 minutes on busy weekdays. The chickpeas add a solid fiber and protein punch without any extra cooking.

product.blurb: Measure your food for two weeks and you will understand your body better than any diet book.
